title: citronella hirsuta
intro: Citronella hirsuta, also known as East Indian citronella, is a perennial grass native to Southeast Asia and India, known for its distinctive citrusy scent and medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of citronella hirsuta include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain and swelling, and promote wound healing. Citronella hirsuta can be prepared into herbal remedies such as essential oils, teas, and infusions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and support overall well-being.
